paper.title: Growth kinetics of starter cultures during refrigerated storage of butter

mechanistic_equations.equation_description: Logistic primary growth model for bacterial concentration over time, assuming no lag phase. The population grows from an initial level toward a maximum carrying capacity with growth rate governed by environmental conditions.

variables.name: time
variables.description: elapsed time since start of growth

variables.name: population at time t
variables.description: logarithmic concentration of the target organism at time t

assumptions: No lag phase; growth begins immediately at the given rate.
Logistic growth with a fixed maximum population density.

source_spans.text_context: Logistic primary growth model for bacterial concentration over time, assuming no lag phase. The population grows from an initial level toward a maximum carrying capacity with growth rate governed by environmental conditions.

mechanistic_equations.equation_description: Describes how the decimal reduction time (D-value) changes with temperature, where z is the temperature increase required to reduce D by one log cycle.
assumptions: Log-linear relationship between D and temperature.
Applicable within the relevant temperature range for the organism.

source_spans.text_context: Describes how the decimal reduction time (D-value) changes with temperature, where z is the temperature increase required to reduce D by one log cycle.

mechanistic_equations.equation_description: Non-linear inactivation model using the Weibull distribution to describe microbial death curves that exhibit tailing or shoulder effects.

parameters.name: scale parameter (time for first log reduction)

assumptions: p = 1 reduces to the log-linear model.

source_spans.text_context: Non-linear inactivation model using the Weibull distribution to describe microbial death curves that exhibit tailing or shoulder effects.
